# Scoutpost — full public AI reference

> AI-powered monitoring infrastructure for journalists and newsrooms. This file is the flattened public AI-facing reference for Scoutpost. It combines the product overview, the public docs summary, and both public skill files in one markdown document.

Last updated: 2026-05-07. Canonical source: https://www.scoutpost.ai/llms-full.txt

---

## 1. Product overview

Scoutpost helps journalists stop re-checking the same pages, feeds, and government sites by hand. It runs scheduled scouts, extracts atomic facts, deduplicates repeated coverage, and keeps the resulting information units in a searchable knowledge base.

The product story exposed on the public site is:

- **Schedule from a chat**: agents can help configure ongoing monitoring.
- **Every story, once**: repeated coverage is collapsed to the underlying facts.
- **A newsroom database, not a chat log**: information units are structured records with sources and timestamps.
- **Works with existing workflows**: results can feed briefs, drafts, and exports.
- **Human verification stays in the loop**: Scoutpost has an explicit editorial approval step.

## 2. What it monitors

| Scout | What it watches | Typical use |
|---|---|---|
| Page Scout | One URL for meaningful changes | press rooms, dockets, filings, policy pages |
| Beat Scout | A beat by topic or geography; weekly/monthly only | housing, procurement, climate, education, towns, districts, regions |
| Social Scout | Social profiles, including deletions | politicians, agencies, campaign accounts |
| Civic Scout | Councils, agendas, minutes, and PDFs | promises, decisions, deadlines, dollar figures |

## 3. Core concepts

- **Scout**: the scheduled monitoring job.
- **Information unit**: one atomic fact linked to its source.
- **Verification**: the human editorial checkpoint that promotes a lead into a citeable fact.
- **Project**: the organizational container for scouts, units, and exports.
- **Credits**: the hosted cost model.

Verification is a hard product boundary. Agents can help search and summarize, but unverified units must be treated as leads rather than publishable facts.

- **MCP**: https://www.scoutpost.ai/mcp

- **MCP bridge docs**: https://github.com/buriedsignals/cojournalist-os/blob/main/mcp/README.md

## 6. Hosted pricing

- Free: 100 credits per month
- Pro: 1,000 credits per month
- Team: 5,000 shared credits per month

Public docs currently describe these per-run costs:

- Page Scout: 1 credit
- Beat Scout: 7 credits; weekly is the highest supported frequency
- Social Scout: 2 credits for most platforms, 15 for Facebook
- Civic Scout: 10 credits in the docs cost table

Agents should disclose spend before running expensive scouts or large batches.

## 7. Public skill: product use

# Scoutpost skill

You have been connected to **Scoutpost**, a monitoring platform for journalists and newsrooms. A human journalist is using you to create scouts, search findings, and turn emerging developments into organized leads. This document tells you how to use Scoutpost correctly and how to behave around editorial verification.

Read this once. Apply it for every Scoutpost task in this session.

## What Scoutpost does

Scoutpost runs scheduled scouts that watch public pages, local news and beats, social profiles, and councils. Each run extracts **information units**: atomic, source-linked facts. Units are deduplicated across repeated coverage and land in an editorial inbox.

The journalist stays responsible for verification. Your job is to help monitor, search, organize, summarize, and draft safely.

## Operational rules

- Do not auto-run expensive operations without confirmation.
- Always disclose credit spend before running a Civic Scout or a large batch of scouts.
- Never present an unverified unit as confirmed fact.
- Always include source URLs when summarizing findings.
- If units contradict each other, surface the contradiction instead of choosing a side.

## Useful URLs

- App: https://www.scoutpost.ai/login
- Docs: https://www.scoutpost.ai/docs
- Docs text: https://www.scoutpost.ai/docs.txt
- Pricing: https://www.scoutpost.ai/pricing
- FAQ: https://www.scoutpost.ai/faq
- Setup skill: https://www.scoutpost.ai/skills/cojournalist-setup.md

Canonical URL: `https://www.scoutpost.ai/skills/cojournalist.md`

Legacy compatibility URL: `https://www.scoutpost.ai/skill.md`

## 8. Public skill: setup

# Scoutpost setup skill

Use this skill when the user wants to deploy, self-host, install, or provision Scoutpost on their own infrastructure.

This is **not** the day-to-day product-use skill. For operating scouts and units inside the hosted product, use:

- https://www.scoutpost.ai/skills/cojournalist.md

## What this skill covers

This setup path walks an agent through:

- preparing the environment
- collecting required API keys
- provisioning Supabase
- deploying edge functions
- building and publishing the frontend
- verifying that the deployment works

## Required external services

- Supabase
- Gemini
- Firecrawl
- Resend
- Apify
- static hosting for the frontend

Canonical URL: `https://www.scoutpost.ai/skills/cojournalist-setup.md`
